que: In Compose, what usually acts as the View?
What is a key role of the ViewModel?
Why does MVVM fit Compose well?
options: Composables can render state from a ViewModel

que: Where should most screen business coordination live?
What is a common MVVM mistake?
options: Putting too much logic in composables

que: What often carries screen data to the UI?
What direction do UI events usually take?
options: From repository straight to XML only

que: Why are ViewModels useful on Android?
options: They are lifecycle-aware screen state holders
They are required for every small composable

que: What should a ViewModel usually avoid?
options: Depending on Android UI widgets directly

que: Which layer normally renders the final pixels?

options: It separates UI rendering from presentation state and coordination
It means putting all logic into one class
It is only for XML layouts
